ogTitle: Tilligerry Creek vs Tilpa: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tilligerry Creek and Tilpa.
heading: Tilligerry Creek vs Tilpa — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tilligerry Creek, New South Wales and Tilpa, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tilpa has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Tilligerry Creek (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tilligerry Creek or Tilpa safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tilligerry Creek has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Tilpa scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tilligerry Creek scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Tilpa scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tilligerry Creek or Tilpa?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tilpa has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
